  "findings": [
    "No fire watch present during hot work",
    "No fire watch maintained during required post-work period"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all hot work immediately",
    "Assign a dedicated fire watch with appropriate extinguishing equipment",
    "Maintain fire watch for at least 30 minutes after hot work ceases, per NFPA 51B",
    "Verify fire watch training and equipment before resuming work"
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926.352 require a fire watch during hot work and for a post-work period (typically 30 minutes) to detect and extinguish smoldering fires. Absence of a fire watch creates an immediate life-safety and property risk, making the work non-compliant and dangerous.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B: Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926.352: Welding and Cutting - Fire prevention"
  ]
}
